steps.title: Identify each cost component
steps.content:
The technician's charge has two parts:
  • Labor: 55 dollars per hour × $x$ hours = $55x$ dollars (variable, depends on time)
  • Parts: a flat 135 dollar fee (constant, regardless of time)

steps.title: Write the total-cost function and evaluate each choice
steps.content:
Total = labor + parts, so $$f(x)=55x+135.$$ Compare to each option:
  • "$f \left(x\right)=x+135$": Charges only 1 dollar per hour for labor instead of 55. Incorrect rate. ✗
  • "$f \left(x\right)=55x$": Captures the hourly labor cost but omits the 135 dollar parts charge entirely. ✗
  • "$f \left(x\right)=55x+135$": 55 dollars per hour for labor plus the 135 dollar parts fee. Matches both components exactly. ✓
  • "$f \left(x\right)=55x-135$": Subtracts 135 instead of adding it. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
